methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Magrathea Metals is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Alex Grant and Jacob Brown that has raised over $100M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of nine same-sector peers: one public company (LNZA) and eight private firms including Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen.

Given the early stage and absence of same-stage or adjacent-stage comparable transactions with sufficient sample size, the peer-median approach was not applicable. A single methodological lens—stage-progression analysis—anchors the valuation. Using the $100M total raised as a proxy baseline and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A-to-subsequent-stage step-ups yields an implied value of $200M. This reflects the step-function capital raise pattern observed in frontier-tech financings at this stage.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $170M to $230M, with a central estimate of $200M. The narrow methodological basis and limited peer comparability within the tight same-stage cohort represent the primary valuation uncertainty. A fuller assessment would benefit from additional comparable transaction data at adjacent stages or from tighter subsector segmentation within climate and energy. The range reflects reasonable variation around the stage-progression anchor rather than independent methodological corroboration.
